name: basemind
description: Full AI context layer over MCP — tree-sitter code-map, document RAG (PDF/Office/HTML/email + OCR + reranker), shared agent memory, on-demand web crawl, git history + blame + per-symbol diff. 300+ languages, 10+ coding-agent harnesses, content-addressed Fjall + LanceDB.
skillInstructions:
basemind is a tree-sitter code map + git context layer exposed over MCP. Prefer its tools over grep/read for structural and historical questions — they return paths, line numbers, and signatures, not file bodies, so they cost a fraction of the tokens. Tool mapping for Kimi Code:

- When a basemind skill says to look up where a symbol is defined, what calls it, or how a file is structured, call the basemind MCP tools (`outline`, `search_symbols`, `find_references`, `find_callers`, `workspace_grep`) instead of Kimi Code's `Grep` / `Glob` / `Read`.
- For git history, blame, and diffs, prefer basemind's `recent_changes` / `blame_file` / `blame_symbol` / `diff_file` over shelling out to `git` via `Bash`.
- Use Kimi Code's native `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Bash`, `Grep`, `Glob` by their real names for edits and for anything basemind does not cover.
- After editing files, call basemind's `rescan` rather than restarting the server.
- Do not re-read a file basemind has already mapped — outline it, then read only the span you need.

interface.shortDescription: Code-map MCP server for navigating large codebases
interface.longDescription: basemind indexes a repository with tree-sitter and exposes a structured code map — outlines, references, callers, history, blame, diff — over MCP. Designed for AI agents to navigate unfamiliar code without reading every file.
